content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Mike and Chocolate Thieves". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/contest/689/problem/C
constraints: 1 <= number of chocolates in each box <= 1000
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer n, which is the number of chocolates. The second line contains n space-separated integers representing the number of chocolates in each box.
content.outputFormat: The minimum number of moves required to make all boxes have the same number of chocolates.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Calculate the median of the chocolates in all boxes and then calculate the total number of moves required to make all boxes have the same number of chocolates as the median.

code.Python3:
import sys
n = int(input())
arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
arr.sort()
median = arr[n // 2]
moves = sum(abs(x - median) for x in arr)
print(moves)
